description: In WasmWrapperGraphBuilder::BuildWasmToJSWrapper (src/compiler/wasm-compiler.cc), the kJSFunctionArityMismatch branch allocates the args SmallVector with size pushed_count + 7, where pushed_count = max(expected_arity, wasm_count - suspend). However, the padding loop 'for (int i = wasm_count - suspend; i < expected_arity; ++i)' starts from wasm_count - suspend, which evaluates to -1 when suspend = kSuspend (1) and wasm_count = 0 (empty parameter list). This causes the loop to execute one extra iteration, pushing an additional undefined_node beyond what was allocated. Consequently, when BuildCallOnCentralStack attempts to write the control() node at index args.size() (index 7 in a size-7 SmallVector), the DCHECK_GT(size(), index) assertion fails (7 > 7). In release builds, this constitutes an out-of-bounds write that corrupts adjacent compiler graph state. The bug is triggered by calling WebAssembly.Function with {suspending: 'first'} and an empty parameter signature while JSPI is enabled (via d8.test.enableJSPI() or --experimental-wasm-jspi), which causes UseGenericWasmToJSWrapper to return false and forces compilation through the vulnerable TurboFan wrapper path.

fixes.description: Backport/adaptation of CL 5662577 for the older JSPI suspend enum shape in this vulnerable image.
fixes.evidence_text: 53d1ddc4ff30d73d15b0b2c1cfe7da04b1a4f906 [wasm][jspi] Add missing type check for suspending WebAssembly.Function
